leads.name: Feminist Press at CUNY
leads.notes: The Feminist Press at CUNY publishes 12-15 titles per year across feminist fiction, poetry, creative nonfiction, memoir, and literature in translation -- a 55-year-old nonprofit with institutional backing from the City University of New York and national distribution. Annual output is consistent and titles are widely reviewed. Outreach angle: their curated annual list of 12-15 titles benefits from a reliable short-run book manufacturer with tight production turnarounds; Cushing-Malloy's Ann Arbor operation has served publishers of this exact profile for generations.

leads.outreach_subject: Short-Run Book Manufacturing for Feminist Press at CUNY

leads.name: Madville Publishing
leads.notes: Madville Publishing is a Texas-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit independent press producing approximately 24 titles per year in poetry, literary essays, memoir, and literary fiction -- one of the highest annual output volumes among small nonprofit literary presses. Their press runs books through Ingram distribution and holds active open submission periods. Outreach angle: at 24 titles per year, Madville needs a short-run book manufacturer that delivers quality at speed; Cushing-Malloy's decades of experience with nonprofit literary press schedules makes this a natural fit.
leads.pitch: Madville Publishing (Lake Dallas, TX) is a 501(c)(3) independent press producing approximately 24 titles per year in poetry, literary fiction, and memoir -- one of the highest output volumes among nonprofit literary presses. Consistent annual manufacturing volume with verified direct contact.
leads.outreach_subject: Short-Run Book Manufacturing for Madville Publishing

leads.name: Persea Books
leads.notes: Persea Books is an independent literary press founded in 1975 and still owned and directed by its founding family -- publishing approximately 10 titles annually in poetry, fiction, essays, memoir, biography, and YA. One of the longest-running fully independent literary publishers in the United States with a backlist of hundreds of titles and national distribution. Outreach angle: a family-owned press with 50 years of independence and a commitment to handcrafted editorial work will appreciate a manufacturing partner with the same values -- quality, personal service, and a long track record.
leads.pitch: Persea Books is a 50-year family-owned independent literary press in New York publishing approximately 10 titles per year in poetry, fiction, essays, memoir, and YA. One of the longest-running fully independent US literary publishers -- a natural fit for Cushing-Malloy's relationship-driven manufacturing services.
leads.outreach_subject: Short-Run Book Manufacturing for Persea Books

leads.name: Kent State University Press
leads.notes: Kent State University Press publishes 18-20 books per year in Civil War history, American studies, Ohio and Rust Belt regional interest, and literary studies -- with institutional budget stability and distribution through the Big Ten Academic Alliance network. Located in Kent, Ohio, approximately 90 miles from Cushing-Malloy in Ann Arbor. leads.name: Loblolly Press
leads.notes: Loblolly Press is a growing Asheville, North Carolina-based independent press founded to publish queer, BIPOC, rural, elder, and neurodivergent writers -- releasing 7 titles in 2025 including 6 books and one anthology, with more than 1,000 books reaching readers in 38 states. Their active Substack newsletter and expanding 2025-2026 catalog signal strong growth. Outreach angle: a growing independent press just hitting meaningful distribution scale needs a manufacturing partner who can scale print runs with them; Cushing-Malloy's short-run capabilities are designed for exactly this stage of publisher growth.

leads.notes: Omnidawn Publishing is an Oakland, California-based nonprofit literary press founded in 2001 and distributed by the University of Chicago Press -- publishing poetry, innovative fiction, and cross-genre literary work with 100+ titles in catalog and an annual poetry prize.
